seat|WD=Q135018062}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京都市山科区西野山岩ヶ谷町1', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 山科神社(西岩屋) {{commons category|Category:Yamashina-jinja}} [[JA:山科神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11470816}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamashina Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamashina-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}, {{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} in {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Uji District|Q10946235}}, {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{jalink|Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{jalink|898|Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{jalink|Engishiki}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{jalink|Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{jalink|Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147}}) and {{jalink|Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{jalink|Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]] [[Category:need translation]]

seat|WD=Q135018062}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京都市山科区西野山岩ヶ谷町1', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 山科神社(西岩屋) {{commons category|Category:Yamashina-jinja}} [[JA:山科神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11470816}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamashina Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamashina-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}, {{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} in {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Uji District|Q10946235}}, {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{jalink|Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{jalink|898|Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{jalink|Engishiki}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{jalink|Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{jalink|Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147}}) and {{jalink|Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{jalink|Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]] [[Category:need translation]]

Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

seat|WD=Q135018062}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京都市山科区西野山岩ヶ谷町1', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 山科神社(西岩屋) {{commons category|Category:Yamashina-jinja}} [[JA:山科神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11470816}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamashina Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamashina-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}, {{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} in {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Uji District|Q10946235}}, {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{jalink|Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{jalink|898|Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{jalink|Engishiki}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{jalink|Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{jalink|Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147}}) and {{jalink|Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{jalink|Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}, {{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} in {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Uji District|Q10946235}}, {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{jalink|Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{jalink|898|Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{jalink|Engishiki}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{jalink|Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{jalink|Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147}}) and {{jalink|Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{jalink|Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Yamashina-jinja}} [[JA:山科神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11470816}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamashina Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamashina-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}, {{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} in {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Uji District|Q10946235}}, {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{jalink|Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{jalink|898|Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{jalink|Engishiki}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{jalink|Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{jalink|Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147}}) and {{jalink|Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{jalink|Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]]

seat|WD=Q135018062}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京都市山科区西野山岩ヶ谷町1', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 山科神社(西岩屋) {{commons category|Category:Yamashina-jinja}} [[JA:山科神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11470816}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamashina Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamashina-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}, {{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} in {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Uji District|Q10946235}}, {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{jalink|Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{jalink|898|Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{jalink|Engishiki}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{jalink|Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{jalink|Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147}}) and {{jalink|Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{jalink|Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]][[Category:qq]]

seat|WD=Q135018062}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京都市山科区西野山岩ヶ谷町1', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 山科神社(西岩屋) {{commons category|Category:Yamashina-jinja}} [[JA:山科神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11470816}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamashina Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamashina-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}{{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{jalink|Yamashina Ward|Q1076993}}, {{jalink|Kyoto|Q34600}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} in {{jalink|Kanpyō|Q1346292}} 9 ({{jalink|897|Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} compiled in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Uji District|Q10946235}}, {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{jalink|Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{jalink|898|Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{jalink|Engishiki}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{jalink|Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{jalink|Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147}}) and {{jalink|Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{jalink|Emperor Daigo|Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{jalink|Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Engi|Q1327274|Engi}} 6 ({{jalink|928|Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{jalink|Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{jalink|Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{jalink|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Yamashina-jinja}} [[JA:山科神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11470816}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamashina Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamashina-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}{{ill|lt=Yamashina Ward|Q1076993|WD=Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill||lt=Yamato Takeru|UNKNOWN}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 9 ({{ill|lt=897|Q30468|WD=Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamashina Ward|Q1076993|WD=Q1076993}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 9 ({{ill|lt=897|Q30468|WD=Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), under {{ill|lt=Uji District|Q10946235|WD=Q10946235}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{ill|lt=Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282|WD=Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 6 ({{ill|lt=928|Q24297|WD=Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{ill|lt=Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401|WD=Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{ill|lt=898|Q24131|WD=Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{ill||lt=Engishiki|UNKNOWN}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488|WD=Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{ill|lt=Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147|WD=Q65262147}}) and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544|WD=Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{ill|lt=Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|WD=Q11049290|lt=Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{ill|lt=Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128|WD=Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 6 ({{ill|lt=928|Q24297|WD=Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{ill|lt=Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282|WD=Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{ill|lt=Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|WD=Q11420603|lt=Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|WD=Q11049290|lt=Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Yamashina-jinja}} [[JA:山科神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11470816}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamashina Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamashina-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}{{ill|lt=Yamashina Ward|Q1076993|WD=Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|UNKNOWN}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 9 ({{ill|lt=897|Q30468|WD=Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamashina Ward|Q1076993|WD=Q1076993}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 9 ({{ill|lt=897|Q30468|WD=Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), under {{ill|lt=Uji District|Q10946235|WD=Q10946235}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{ill|lt=Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282|WD=Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 6 ({{ill|lt=928|Q24297|WD=Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{ill|lt=Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401|WD=Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{ill|lt=898|Q24131|WD=Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|UNKNOWN}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488|WD=Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{ill|lt=Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147|WD=Q65262147}}) and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544|WD=Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{ill|lt=Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|WD=Q11049290|lt=Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{ill|lt=Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128|WD=Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 6 ({{ill|lt=928|Q24297|WD=Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{ill|lt=Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282|WD=Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{ill|lt=Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|WD=Q11420603|lt=Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|WD=Q11049290|lt=Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Yamashina-jinja}} [[JA:山科神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11470816}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamashina Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yamashina-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}{{ill|lt=Yamashina Ward|Q1076993|WD=Q1076993}}Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}}
Wakatake-no-Ō |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 9 ({{ill|lt=897|Q30468|WD=Q30468}}) |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} (Yamashina Festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|label=Yamashina Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamashina-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Yamashina Shrine''' (やましなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Nishinoyama Iwagaya-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamashina Ward|Q1076993|WD=Q1076993}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following twoExplanatory board in the precincts.. :* {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} (やまとたけるのみこと) :* Wakatake-no-Ō (わかたけのみこと) - son of Yamato Takeru. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the number of enshrined seats is recorded as two. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says it was founded by imperial command of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} in {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 9 ({{ill|lt=897|Q30468|WD=Q30468}}). It is said to have been the ancestral deity of the local powerful Miyamichi clan. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), under {{ill|lt=Uji District|Q10946235|WD=Q10946235}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276}}, it is listed as "two seats of Yamashina Shrine, both Myojin major, receiving offerings at monthly and niiname rites," indicating both seats ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and received court offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Also, in ''{{ill|lt=Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282|WD=Q11076282}}'', it is recorded that in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 6 ({{ill|lt=928|Q24297|WD=Q24297}}), "Yamashina Ōkami" was promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Documents also attest to the shrine festival "'''Yamashina Festival'''". ''{{ill|lt=Honchō Gatsuryō|Q11520401|WD=Q11520401}}''''Honchō Gatsuryō'', upper third month, Yamashina festival entry. states that in Kanpyō 10 ({{ill|lt=898|Q24131|WD=Q24131}}) the festival became an official court festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}}''Engishiki'' vol.15 (Kuraryō), Yamashina Festival entry. records that it was held annually on the day of the first snake of the 4th and 11th months. Since {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Taneko|Q11623488|WD=Q11623488}}, born to Miyamichi no Retsuko (daughter of {{ill|lt=Miyamichi no Yasumasu|Q65262147|WD=Q65262147}}) and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Takafuji|Q5507544|WD=Q5507544}}, became the birth mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753}} (reigned from Kanpyō 9 [897] to Engi 8 [930]), it has been pointed out that the Miyamichi clan's position as the emperor's maternal relatives was the background for official designation of the Yamashina Festival{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. However, the "Yamashina deity" in these sources is also theorized to refer instead to {{ill|lt=Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|WD=Q11049290|lt=Iwaya Shrine}} (Ōyake Nakakōji-chō, Yamashina Ward) or Miyamichi Shrine (Kanjūji Niōdō-chō, Yamashina Ward), and the matter is not settled{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. Iwaya Shrine is said to have been this shrine's inner shrine (now independent), and when {{ill|lt=Ōishi Yoshio|Q530128|WD=Q530128}} lived in concealment in Yamashina, he is said to have secluded himself at Iwaya Shrine to pray for fulfillment of his great aim. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called "Nishi-Iwaya Daimyōjin" or "Ichinomiya". After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, the shrine was renamed to the present "Yamashina Shrine"{{Sfn|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 6 ({{ill|lt=928|Q24297|WD=Q24297}}) November 9: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower (''{{ill|lt=Fusō Ryakki|Q11076282|WD=Q11076282}}'')''Fusō Ryakki'', supplementary notes, entry for Nov 9, Engi 6. - see [{{NDLDC|991096/355}} ''Kokushi Taikei Vol.6 Nihon Itsushi / Fusō Ryakki''] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frame 355. - written as "Yamashina Ōkami". === Precincts === File:Yamashina-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Honden (Kyoto City designated cultural property) File:Yamashina-jinja, haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Yamashina-jinja, shamusho.jpg|Shrine office / temizuya File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Yamashina-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Honden, 1 building (with 2 stone lanterns) (architecture) - built in the early Edo period. Designated June 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59)[http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000005774.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture - Yamashina Ward], [http://www.city.kyoto.lg.jp/bunshi/page/0000189341.html Kyoto City designated/registered cultural properties - architecture (Yamashina) > Yamashina Shrine Honden] (Kyoto City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === References === * Precinct explanatory board (installed by Kyoto City) * {{Cite book|和書|author=Katō Takahisa|authorlink=Katō Takahisa|chapter=Yamashina Festival|year=|title={{ill|lt=Kokushi Daijiten (Shōwa era)|Q11420603|WD=Q11420603|lt=Kokushi Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Festival (Kokushi Daijiten)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=Yamashina Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 27 Place Names of Kyoto City|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910122|ref={{Harvid|Yamashina Shrine (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Iwaya Shrine (Kyoto)|Q11049290|WD=Q11049290|lt=Iwaya Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|016002|Two seats of Yamashina Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やましなしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamashina Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名やましなしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:宇治郡]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
